short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value: To develop the student's skills in the design and analysis of algorithms; To study algorithms for a range of important standard problems; To introduce the student to the theory of NP-completeness together with its practical implications;To make the student aware of fundamental concepts of computability.

requirements_of_entry.label: Requirements of Entry
value: 40 credits of Level 1 Mathematics is strongly recommended.
This course is only available to Honours students.

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value: Two one-hour lectures and one one-hour tutorial per week.

course_aims.label: Course Aims

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course

description_of_summative_assessment.label: Description of Summative Assessment
description_of_summative_assessment.value: Examination 75%, assessed practical exercise 20% and in-class quizzes 5%.
